name: Bunny Briggs
abstract: Bunny Briggs (February 26, 1922 – November 15, 2014) was an American tap dancer who was inducted into the American Tap Dancing Hall of Fame in 2006. Briggs was born under the name Bernard Briggs in Harlem, New York on February 26, 1922.

about_quotes.speaker: Cliff Leeman
about_quotes.evidence: Bunny Briggs was with us, played 'East Side, West Side, All Around The Town'... Bunny Briggs was with us for a long time, stumping for some of the acts.

about_quotes.speaker: Louie Bellson
about_quotes.evidence: That’s how Baby Lawrence, Teddy Hale, Bunny Briggs – that’s where all those guys came up, from the tap-dance era.
about_quotes.doc_title: Louie Bellson — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Chuck Green
about_quotes.evidence: That's Bunny Briggs. And another great friend, you see, but we lost him, you see.

about_quotes.speaker: Butch Miles
about_quotes.evidence: He had the whole band there, Bunny Briggs, the great tap dancer was there
about_quotes.doc_title: Butch Miles interviewed by Monk Rowe and Michael Woods, Scottsdale, Arizona, March 4, 1995 [interview 1 of 2]

about_quotes.speaker: Trummy Young
about_quotes.evidence: I remember Bunny Briggs when he was a young kid doing those things back there then. And the strange thing about Bunny, I worked with Charlie Barnett, and Bunny was dancing with that band for a while.
